reason: Adding the noexec mount option to /dev/shm is a straightforward configuration change that does not affect authentication, networking, or critical boot parameters. It can be applied by editing /etc/fstab and remounting the filesystem, with minimal risk of locking out access.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; execution of binaries from world-writable temporary storage; possible disruption of applications that rely on executing code from /dev/shm
